channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: At Extreme Strain Rates, Pure Metals Thermally Harden while Alloys Thermally Soften
item.description:
Author(s): Ian Dowding and Christopher A. Schuh

When materials are deformed at extreme strain rates, $>{10}^{6}\text{ }\text{ }{\mathrm{s}}^{−1}$, a counterintuitive mechanical response is seen where the strength and hardness of pure metals increases with increasing temperature. This antithermal hardening is due to dislocations meeting resista…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076101] Published Tue Feb 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ian Dowding and Christopher A. Schuh

When materials are deformed at extreme strain rates, >106  s−1, a counterintuitive mechanical response is seen where the strength and hardness of pure metals increases with increasing temperature. This antithermal hardening is due to dislocations meeting resistance to their motion from phonons in…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076101] Published Tue Feb 17, 2026
item.dc:title: At Extreme Strain Rates, Pure Metals Thermally Harden while Alloys Thermally Soften
item.dc:creator: Ian Dowding and Christopher A. Schuh
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Observation of Robust Macroscale Structural Superlubricity
item.description:
Author(s): Minhao Han, Deli Peng, Dinglin Yang, Jin Wang, Yi Zheng, Guofeng Hu, Yifan Shao, Jiaying Li, Feng Ding, Zhiping Xu, Michael Urbakh, and Quanshui Zheng

Robust macroscale structural superlubricity is demonstrated within a single submillimeter graphite contact.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076201] Published Tue Feb 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Minhao Han, Deli Peng, Dinglin Yang, Jin Wang, Yi Zheng, Guofeng Hu, Yifan Shao, Jiaying Li, Feng Ding, Zhiping Xu, Michael Urbakh, and Quanshui Zheng

Robust macroscale structural superlubricity is demonstrated within a single submillimeter graphite contact.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076201] Published Tue Feb 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Observation of Robust Macroscale Structural Superlubricity
item.dc:creator: Minhao Han, Deli Peng, Dinglin Yang, Jin Wang, Yi Zheng, Guofeng Hu, Yifan Shao, Jiaying Li, Feng Ding, Zhiping Xu, Michael Urbakh, and Quanshui Zheng
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Extremely High Excitonic $g$ Factors in 2D Crystals by Alloy-Induced Admixing of Band States
item.description:
Author(s): Katarzyna Olkowska-Pucko et al.

Semiconducting transition metal dichalcogenide monolayer alloys provide a material platform with remarkable valley-tunable properties that can be manipulated on demand.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076901] Published Tue Feb 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Katarzyna Olkowska-Pucko et al.

Semiconducting transition metal dichalcogenide monolayer alloys provide a material platform with remarkable valley-tunable properties that can be manipulated on demand.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076901] Published Tue Feb 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Extremely High Excitonic $g$ Factors in 2D Crystals by Alloy-Induced Admixing of Band States
item.dc:creator: Katarzyna Olkowska-Pucko et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Composite Fermion Theory of Fractional Chern Insulator Stability
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaodong Hu, Ying Ran, and Di Xiao

A mean-field theory of fractional Chern insulators based on the dipole picture of composite fermions extends beyond the ideal-band limit.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066504] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaodong Hu, Ying Ran, and Di Xiao

A mean-field theory of fractional Chern insulators based on the dipole picture of composite fermions extends beyond the ideal-band limit.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066504] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Composite Fermion Theory of Fractional Chern Insulator Stability
item.dc:creator: Xiaodong Hu, Ying Ran, and Di Xiao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Floquet Engineering Spin Triplet States in Unconventional Magnets
item.description:
Author(s): Pei-Hao Fu, Sayan Mondal, Jun-Feng Liu, Yukio Tanaka, and Jorge Cayao

High-frequency linearly polarized light generates spin-triplet densities and odd-frequency spin-triplet Cooper pairs in d-wave altermagnets, features absent in the static phase.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066703] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pei-Hao Fu, Sayan Mondal, Jun-Feng Liu, Yukio Tanaka, and Jorge Cayao

High-frequency linearly polarized light generates spin-triplet densities and odd-frequency spin-triplet Cooper pairs in d-wave altermagnets, features absent in the static phase.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066703] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Floquet Engineering Spin Triplet States in Unconventional Magnets
item.dc:creator: Pei-Hao Fu, Sayan Mondal, Jun-Feng Liu, Yukio Tanaka, and Jorge Cayao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Bosonization of Noise Effects in Nonlocal Quantum Dynamics
item.description:
Author(s): Michele Fantechi and Marco Merkli

Quantum systems that interact nonlocally with an environment are paradigms for exploring collective phenomena. They naturally emerge in various physical contexts involving long-range, many-body interactions. We consider a general class of such open systems characterized by a coupling to the environm…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 060402] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Michele Fantechi and Marco Merkli

Quantum systems that interact nonlocally with an environment are paradigms for exploring collective phenomena. They naturally emerge in various physical contexts involving long-range, many-body interactions. We consider a general class of such open systems characterized by a coupling to the environm…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 060402] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Bosonization of Noise Effects in Nonlocal Quantum Dynamics
item.dc:creator: Michele Fantechi and Marco Merkli
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Universal Crossover in the Three-Channel Charge Kondo Model at High Transparency
item.description:
Author(s): Nicolas Paris, Nicolas Dupuis, and Christophe Mora

Quantum impurity models provide a central framework for correlated electron physics, with quantum dots enabling controlled experimental realizations. While their weak-coupling behavior is well understood through mappings to Kondo Hamiltonians, the opposite regime of highly transparent contacts has l…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066501] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nicolas Paris, Nicolas Dupuis, and Christophe Mora

Quantum impurity models provide a central framework for correlated electron physics, with quantum dots enabling controlled experimental realizations. While their weak-coupling behavior is well understood through mappings to Kondo Hamiltonians, the opposite regime of highly transparent contacts has l…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066501] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Universal Crossover in the Three-Channel Charge Kondo Model at High Transparency
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Paris, Nicolas Dupuis, and Christophe Mora
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quasi-One-Dimensional Spin Excitations in the Iron Pnictide ${\mathrm{NaFe}}_{0.53}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{0.47}\mathrm{As}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yifan Wang, David W. Tam, Weiyi Wang, R. A. Ewings, J. Ross Stewart, Masaaki Matsuda, Chongde Cao, Changle Liu, Rong Yu, Pengcheng Dai, and Yu Song

Spectroscopic measurements in model 1D correlated systems offer insights for understanding their two-dimensional counterparts, which include the cuprate and iron pnictide/chalcogenide superconductors. A major challenge is the identification of such correlated systems with dominantly 1D physics. In t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066503] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yifan Wang, David W. Tam, Weiyi Wang, R. A. Ewings, J. Ross Stewart, Masaaki Matsuda, Chongde Cao, Changle Liu, Rong Yu, Pengcheng Dai, and Yu Song

Spectroscopic measurements in model 1D correlated systems offer insights for understanding their two-dimensional counterparts, which include the cuprate and iron pnictide/chalcogenide superconductors. A major challenge is the identification of such correlated systems with dominantly 1D physics. In t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066503] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Quasi-One-Dimensional Spin Excitations in the Iron Pnictide ${\mathrm{NaFe}}_{0.53}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{0.47}\mathrm{As}$
item.dc:creator: Yifan Wang, David W. Tam, Weiyi Wang, R. A. Ewings, J. Ross Stewart, Masaaki Matsuda, Chongde Cao, Changle Liu, Rong Yu, Pengcheng Dai, and Yu Song

item.title: Cross-Process Interference in Single-Cycle Electron Emission from Metal Needle Tips
item.description:
Author(s): Anne Herzig, Peter Hommelhoff, Eleftherios Goulielmakis, Thomas Fennel, and Lennart Seiffert

Though interference from different emission channels enabled a deeper understanding of strong-field photoemission in atoms and molecules, it remained out of reach for solids. Here, we explore metal needle tips under single-cycle pulses via classical trajectories extended by quantum interference and …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066904] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Anne Herzig, Peter Hommelhoff, Eleftherios Goulielmakis, Thomas Fennel, and Lennart Seiffert

Though interference from different emission channels enabled a deeper understanding of strong-field photoemission in atoms and molecules, it remained out of reach for solids. Here, we explore metal needle tips under single-cycle pulses via classical trajectories extended by quantum interference and …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066904] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Cross-Process Interference in Single-Cycle Electron Emission from Metal Needle Tips
item.dc:creator: Anne Herzig, Peter Hommelhoff, Eleftherios Goulielmakis, Thomas Fennel, and Lennart Seiffert
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Cavity Controls Core-to-Core Resonant Inelastic X-Ray Scattering
item.description:
Author(s): S.-X. Wang, Z.-Q. Zhao, X.-Y. Wang, T.-J. Li, Y. Su, Y. Uemura, F. Alves Lima, A. Khadiev, B.-H. Wang, J. M. Ablett, J-P. Rueff, H.-C. Wang, O. J. L. Fox, W.-B. Li, L.-F. Zhu, and X.-C. Huang

X-ray cavity quantum optics with inner-shell transitions has been limited by the spectral overlap between resonant and continuum states. Here, we report the first experimental demonstration of cavity-controlled core-to-core resonant inelastic x-ray scattering (RIXS). We suppress the absorption-edge …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 063601] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): S.-X. Wang, Z.-Q. Zhao, X.-Y. Wang, T.-J. Li, Y. Su, Y. Uemura, F. Alves Lima, A. Khadiev, B.-H. Wang, J. M. Ablett, J-P. Rueff, H.-C. Wang, O. J. L. Fox, W.-B. Li, L.-F. Zhu, and X.-C. Huang

X-ray cavity quantum optics with inner-shell transitions has been limited by the spectral overlap between resonant and continuum states. Here, we report the first experimental demonstration of cavity-controlled core-to-core resonant inelastic x-ray scattering (RIXS). We suppress the absorption-edge …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 063601] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Cavity Controls Core-to-Core Resonant Inelastic X-Ray Scattering
item.dc:creator: S.-X. Wang, Z.-Q. Zhao, X.-Y. Wang, T.-J. Li, Y. Su, Y. Uemura, F. Alves Lima, A. Khadiev, B.-H. Wang, J. M. Ablett, J-P. Rueff, H.-C. Wang, O. J. L. Fox, W.-B. Li, L.-F. Zhu, and X.-C. Huang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Parity Breaking and Sublattice Dichotomy in Monolayer FeSe Superconductor
item.description:
Author(s): Cui Ding, Zhipeng Xu, Xiaotong Jiao, Yinqi Hu, Wenxuan Zhao, Lexian Yang, Kun Jiang, Lili Wang, Jin-Feng Jia, Jiangping Hu, and Qi-Kun Xue

A unit cell represents the smallest repeating structure in solid-state physics and serves as the fundamental building block of a material. In iron-based superconductors, each unit cell contains two iron atoms, which form two sublattices in the two-dimensional iron layers. Under normal circumstances,…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066502] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Cui Ding, Zhipeng Xu, Xiaotong Jiao, Yinqi Hu, Wenxuan Zhao, Lexian Yang, Kun Jiang, Lili Wang, Jin-Feng Jia, Jiangping Hu, and Qi-Kun Xue

A unit cell represents the smallest repeating structure in solid-state physics and serves as the fundamental building block of a material. In iron-based superconductors, each unit cell contains two iron atoms, which form two sublattices in the two-dimensional iron layers. Under normal circumstances,…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066502] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Parity Breaking and Sublattice Dichotomy in Monolayer FeSe Superconductor
item.dc:creator: Cui Ding, Zhipeng Xu, Xiaotong Jiao, Yinqi Hu, Wenxuan Zhao, Lexian Yang, Kun Jiang, Lili Wang, Jin-Feng Jia, Jiangping Hu, and Qi-Kun Xue
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Magnetoelastic Coupling-Driven Chiral Spin Textures: A Skyrmion-Antiskyrmion-like Array
item.description:
Author(s): Gyungchoon Go and Se Kwon Kim

We theoretically demonstrate that sufficiently strong magnetoelastic coupling can change the ground state of otherwise uniform spin systems to chiral spin configurations. More specifically, we show that a periodic array of chiral spin textures can spontaneously emerge in a two-dimensional ferromagne…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066702] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gyungchoon Go and Se Kwon Kim

We theoretically demonstrate that sufficiently strong magnetoelastic coupling can change the ground state of otherwise uniform spin systems to chiral spin configurations. More specifically, we show that a periodic array of chiral spin textures can spontaneously emerge in a two-dimensional ferromagne…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066702] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetoelastic Coupling-Driven Chiral Spin Textures: A Skyrmion-Antiskyrmion-like Array
item.dc:creator: Gyungchoon Go and Se Kwon Kim
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Enhancement of Indistinguishable-Photon Emission from a GaAs Quantum Dot via Charge-Noise Suppression
item.description:
Author(s): Priyabrata Mudi, Avijit Barua, Kartik Gaur, Steffen Wilksen, Alexander Steinhoff, Setthanat Wijitpatima, Sarthak Tripathi, Julian Ritzmann, Andreas D. Wieck, Sven Rodt, Christopher Gies, Arne Ludwig, and Stephan Reitzenstein

The generation of indistinguishable single photons is a fundamental requirement for photonic quantum technologies. However, spectral fluctuations, often induced by charge noise in epitaxial quantum dots (QDs), lead to exciton dephasing, thereby limiting their practical usage in quantum applications.…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066903] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Priyabrata Mudi, Avijit Barua, Kartik Gaur, Steffen Wilksen, Alexander Steinhoff, Setthanat Wijitpatima, Sarthak Tripathi, Julian Ritzmann, Andreas D. Wieck, Sven Rodt, Christopher Gies, Arne Ludwig, and Stephan Reitzenstein

The generation of indistinguishable single photons is a fundamental requirement for photonic quantum technologies. However, spectral fluctuations, often induced by charge noise in epitaxial quantum dots (QDs), lead to exciton dephasing, thereby limiting their practical usage in quantum applications.…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066903] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Enhancement of Indistinguishable-Photon Emission from a GaAs Quantum Dot via Charge-Noise Suppression
item.dc:creator: Priyabrata Mudi, Avijit Barua, Kartik Gaur, Steffen Wilksen, Alexander Steinhoff, Setthanat Wijitpatima, Sarthak Tripathi, Julian Ritzmann, Andreas D. Wieck, Sven Rodt, Christopher Gies, Arne Ludwig, and Stephan Reitzenstein
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Shear-Rate Dependent Surface Tension of Glass-Forming Fluids
item.description:
Author(s): Linnea Heitmeier and Thomas Voigtmann

We investigate the interface of a glass-forming fluid showing non-Newtonian rheology. By applying shear flow in the interface, we observe that the surface tension depends on the shear rate. Importantly, the standard way of determining surface tension from the pressure anisotropy caused by the interf…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 068203] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Linnea Heitmeier and Thomas Voigtmann

We investigate the interface of a glass-forming fluid showing non-Newtonian rheology. By applying shear flow in the interface, we observe that the surface tension depends on the shear rate. Importantly, the standard way of determining surface tension from the pressure anisotropy caused by the interf…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 068203] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Shear-Rate Dependent Surface Tension of Glass-Forming Fluids
item.dc:creator: Linnea Heitmeier and Thomas Voigtmann
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Polymers, Chemical Physics, Soft Matter, and Biological Physics
item.prism:section: Polymers, Chemical Physics, Soft Matter, and Biological Physics

item.title: Gravity-Driven Flux of Particles through Apertures
item.description:
Author(s): Ram Sudhir Sharma, Alexandre Leonelli, Kevin Zhao, Eckart Meiburg, and Alban Sauret

The gravity-driven discharge of granular material through an aperture is a fundamental problem in granular physics and is classically described by empirical laws with different fitting parameters. In this Letter, we disentangle the mass flux into distinct velocity and packing contributions by combin…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 068204] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ram Sudhir Sharma, Alexandre Leonelli, Kevin Zhao, Eckart Meiburg, and Alban Sauret

The gravity-driven discharge of granular material through an aperture is a fundamental problem in granular physics and is classically described by empirical laws with different fitting parameters. In this Letter, we disentangle the mass flux into distinct velocity and packing contributions by combin…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 068204] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Gravity-Driven Flux of Particles through Apertures
item.dc:creator: Ram Sudhir Sharma, Alexandre Leonelli, Kevin Zhao, Eckart Meiburg, and Alban Sauret
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Polymers, Chemical Physics, Soft Matter, and Biological Physics

item.title: Quantum Storage with Flat Bands
item.description:
Author(s): Carlo Danieli, Jie Liu, Rudolf A. Römer, and Rodrigo A. Vicencio

The realization of robust quantum storage devices relies on the ability to generate long-lived, spatially localized states. In this Letter, we introduce a method for the targeted creation of compact excitations in flat-band lattices. By injecting in-plane radiation waves from the system’s edge and a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066302] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Carlo Danieli, Jie Liu, Rudolf A. Römer, and Rodrigo A. Vicencio

The realization of robust quantum storage devices relies on the ability to generate long-lived, spatially localized states. In this Letter, we introduce a method for the targeted creation of compact excitations in flat-band lattices. By injecting in-plane radiation waves from the system’s edge and a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066302] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Storage with Flat Bands
item.dc:creator: Carlo Danieli, Jie Liu, Rudolf A. Römer, and Rodrigo A. Vicencio
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Marginal Metals and Kosterlitz-Thouless-Type Phase Transition in Disordered Altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Chang-An Li, Bo Fu, Huaiming Guo, Björn Trauzettel, and Song-Bo Zhang

Altermagnetism, a recently discovered magnetic phase characterized by spin-split bands without net magnetization, has emerged as a promising platform for novel physics and potential applications. However, its stability against disorder—ubiquitous in real materials—remains poorly understood. Here, we…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066303] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chang-An Li, Bo Fu, Huaiming Guo, Björn Trauzettel, and Song-Bo Zhang

Altermagnetism, a recently discovered magnetic phase characterized by spin-split bands without net magnetization, has emerged as a promising platform for novel physics and potential applications. However, its stability against disorder—ubiquitous in real materials—remains poorly understood. Here, we…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066303] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Marginal Metals and Kosterlitz-Thouless-Type Phase Transition in Disordered Altermagnets
item.dc:creator: Chang-An Li, Bo Fu, Huaiming Guo, Björn Trauzettel, and Song-Bo Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Ultrasensitive Magnetometer Based on Cusp Points of the Photon-Magnon Synchronization Mode
item.description:
Author(s): Xinlin Mi, Jinwei Rao, Lijun Yan, Xudong Wang, Bingbing Lyu, Bimu Yao, Shishen Yan, and Lihui Bai

Ultrasensitive magnetometers based on spin resonances have led to remarkable achievements. However, the field responsivities of these spin resonances are inherently constrained by these particles’ gyromagnetic ratios, such as the electron, with a constant gyromagnetic ratio of ${γ}_{e}=2π×28\text{ }…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066701] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xinlin Mi, Jinwei Rao, Lijun Yan, Xudong Wang, Bingbing Lyu, Bimu Yao, Shishen Yan, and Lihui Bai

Ultrasensitive magnetometers based on spin resonances have led to remarkable achievements. However, the field responsivities of these spin resonances are inherently constrained by these particles’ gyromagnetic ratios, such as the electron, with a constant gyromagnetic ratio of γe=2π×28  GHz/T. Here,…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066701] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Ultrasensitive Magnetometer Based on Cusp Points of the Photon-Magnon Synchronization Mode
item.dc:creator: Xinlin Mi, Jinwei Rao, Lijun Yan, Xudong Wang, Bingbing Lyu, Bimu Yao, Shishen Yan, and Lihui Bai
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Pressure-Tunable Hyperbolic Plasmons in Black Phosphorus Films
item.description:
Author(s): Yuwei Liu, Chong Wang, Junwei Ma, Yuqing Zheng, Wenqi Bi, Hao Sun, Xiangkai Meng, Shenyang Huang, Xiang Li, Hugen Yan, and Yugui Yao

High-pressure environments provide a unique platform for tuning quantum phenomena, yet their applications in plasmonics remain underexplored. Here, we investigate the pressure-induced evolution of plasmons in black phosphorus films using infrared spectroscopy. Continuous pressure tuning of anisotrop…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066902] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuwei Liu, Chong Wang, Junwei Ma, Yuqing Zheng, Wenqi Bi, Hao Sun, Xiangkai Meng, Shenyang Huang, Xiang Li, Hugen Yan, and Yugui Yao

High-pressure environments provide a unique platform for tuning quantum phenomena, yet their applications in plasmonics remain underexplored. Here, we investigate the pressure-induced evolution of plasmons in black phosphorus films using infrared spectroscopy. Continuous pressure tuning of anisotrop…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066902] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Pressure-Tunable Hyperbolic Plasmons in Black Phosphorus Films
item.dc:creator: Yuwei Liu, Chong Wang, Junwei Ma, Yuqing Zheng, Wenqi Bi, Hao Sun, Xiangkai Meng, Shenyang Huang, Xiang Li, Hugen Yan, and Yugui Yao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Unlocking Hidden Topological Multistability via Biphasic Correlated Order Evolution
item.description:
Author(s): Jin-Bing Wu, Zhenghao Guo, Baoming Shi, Daoxing Luo, Lei Zhang, Yan-Qing Lu, and Wei Hu

Topological multistability reflects the complexity of structure evolution inside ordered condensed matter. For a given thermodynamic system, the actual attained stable states decline sharply compared with the theoretical anticipation, which severely restricts the diversity of the material structure …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 068101] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jin-Bing Wu, Zhenghao Guo, Baoming Shi, Daoxing Luo, Lei Zhang, Yan-Qing Lu, and Wei Hu

Topological multistability reflects the complexity of structure evolution inside ordered condensed matter. For a given thermodynamic system, the actual attained stable states decline sharply compared with the theoretical anticipation, which severely restricts the diversity of the material structure …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 068101] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Unlocking Hidden Topological Multistability via Biphasic Correlated Order Evolution
item.dc:creator: Jin-Bing Wu, Zhenghao Guo, Baoming Shi, Daoxing Luo, Lei Zhang, Yan-Qing Lu, and Wei Hu

item.title: Quantum Charging Advantage in Superconducting Solid-State Batteries
item.description:
Author(s): Chang-Kang Hu, Chilong Liu, Jingchao Zhao, Liuzhu Zhong, Yuxuan Zhou, Mingze Liu, Haolan Yuan, Yongchang Lin, Yue Xu, Guantian Hu, Guixu Xie, Zixing Liu, Ruiyang Zhou, Yougui Ri, Wenxuan Zhang, Ruicheng Deng, Andreia Saguia, Xiayu Linpeng, Marcelo S. Sarandy, Song Liu, Alan C. Santos, Dian Tan, and Dapeng Yu

Quantum battery, as a novel energy storage device, offers the potential for unprecedented efficiency and performance beyond the capabilities of classical systems, with broad implications for future quantum technologies. Here, we experimentally demonstrate quantum charging advantage (QCA) in a scalab…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 060401] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chang-Kang Hu, Chilong Liu, Jingchao Zhao, Liuzhu Zhong, Yuxuan Zhou, Mingze Liu, Haolan Yuan, Yongchang Lin, Yue Xu, Guantian Hu, Guixu Xie, Zixing Liu, Ruiyang Zhou, Yougui Ri, Wenxuan Zhang, Ruicheng Deng, Andreia Saguia, Xiayu Linpeng, Marcelo S. Sarandy, Song Liu, Alan C. Santos, Dian Tan, and Dapeng Yu

Quantum battery, as a novel energy storage device, offers the potential for unprecedented efficiency and performance beyond the capabilities of classical systems, with broad implications for future quantum technologies. Here, we experimentally demonstrate quantum charging advantage (QCA) in a scalab…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 060401] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Charging Advantage in Superconducting Solid-State Batteries
item.dc:creator: Chang-Kang Hu, Chilong Liu, Jingchao Zhao, Liuzhu Zhong, Yuxuan Zhou, Mingze Liu, Haolan Yuan, Yongchang Lin, Yue Xu, Guantian Hu, Guixu Xie, Zixing Liu, Ruiyang Zhou, Yougui Ri, Wenxuan Zhang, Ruicheng Deng, Andreia Saguia, Xiayu Linpeng, Marcelo S. Sarandy, Song Liu, Alan C. Santos, Dian Tan, and Dapeng Yu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Exact Chiral Symmetries of $3+1\mathrm{D}$ Hamiltonian Lattice Fermions
item.description:
Author(s): Lei Gioia and Ryan Thorngren

We construct Hamiltonian models on a $3+1\mathrm{D}$ cubic lattice for a single Weyl fermion and for a single Weyl doublet protected by exact (as opposed to emergent) chiral symmetries. In the former, we find a not-on-site, noncompact chiral symmetry which can be viewed as a Hamiltonian analog of th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 061601] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lei Gioia and Ryan Thorngren

We construct Hamiltonian models on a 3+1D cubic lattice for a single Weyl fermion and for a single Weyl doublet protected by exact (as opposed to emergent) chiral symmetries. In the former, we find a not-on-site, noncompact chiral symmetry which can be viewed as a Hamiltonian analog of the Ginsparg-…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 061601] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Exact Chiral Symmetries of $3+1\mathrm{D}$ Hamiltonian Lattice Fermions
item.dc:creator: Lei Gioia and Ryan Thorngren
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Particles and Fields

item.title: Muon Knight Shift as a Precise Probe of the Superconducting Symmetry of ${\mathrm{Sr}}_{2}{\mathrm{RuO}}_{4}$
item.description:
Author(s): Hisakazu Matsuki, Rustem Khasanov, Jonas A. Krieger, Thomas J. Hicken, Kosuke Yuchi, Jake S. Bobowski, Giordano Mattoni, Atsutoshi Ikeda, Ryutaro Okuma, Hubertus Luetkens, and Yoshiteru Maeno

Muon spin rotation ($μ\mathrm{SR}$) measurements of internal magnetic field shifts, known as the muon Knight shift, are used for determining pairing symmetries in superconductors. While this technique has been especially effective for $f$-electron-based heavy-fermion superconductors, it remains chal…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066001] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hisakazu Matsuki, Rustem Khasanov, Jonas A. Krieger, Thomas J. Hicken, Kosuke Yuchi, Jake S. Bobowski, Giordano Mattoni, Atsutoshi Ikeda, Ryutaro Okuma, Hubertus Luetkens, and Yoshiteru Maeno

Muon spin rotation (μSR) measurements of internal magnetic field shifts, known as the muon Knight shift, are used for determining pairing symmetries in superconductors. While this technique has been especially effective for f-electron-based heavy-fermion superconductors, it remains challenging in d-…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066001] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Muon Knight Shift as a Precise Probe of the Superconducting Symmetry of ${\mathrm{Sr}}_{2}{\mathrm{RuO}}_{4}$
item.dc:creator: Hisakazu Matsuki, Rustem Khasanov, Jonas A. Krieger, Thomas J. Hicken, Kosuke Yuchi, Jake S. Bobowski, Giordano Mattoni, Atsutoshi Ikeda, Ryutaro Okuma, Hubertus Luetkens, and Yoshiteru Maeno
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Superconducting Dome in ${\mathrm{La}}_{3−x}{\mathrm{Sr}}_{x}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7−δ}$ Thin Films
item.description:
Author(s): Maosen Wang, Bo Hao, Wenjie Sun, Shengjun Yan, Shengwang Sun, Hongyi Zhang, Zhengbin Gu, and Yuefeng Nie

The doping-dependent phase diagram of the bilayer nickelate film, La3−xSrxNi2O7−δ, when compressively strained features a superconducting dome with an electron-hole crossover.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066002] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Maosen Wang, Bo Hao, Wenjie Sun, Shengjun Yan, Shengwang Sun, Hongyi Zhang, Zhengbin Gu, and Yuefeng Nie

The doping-dependent phase diagram of the bilayer nickelate film, La3−xSrxNi2O7−δ, when compressively strained features a superconducting dome with an electron-hole crossover.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066002] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Superconducting Dome in ${\mathrm{La}}_{3−x}{\mathrm{Sr}}_{x}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7−δ}$ Thin Films
item.dc:creator: Maosen Wang, Bo Hao, Wenjie Sun, Shengjun Yan, Shengwang Sun, Hongyi Zhang, Zhengbin Gu, and Yuefeng Nie
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Frequency-Domain Berry Curvature Effect on Time Refraction
item.description:
Author(s): Shiyue Deng, Yang Gao, and Qian Niu

We demonstrate that there exists frequency-domain Berry curvature in the wave function of photons in dispersive optical systems. This property arises from the frequency dispersion of its dielectric function, which makes Maxwell equations a nonstandard eigenvalue equation, with the eigenvalue (freque…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066301] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shiyue Deng, Yang Gao, and Qian Niu

We demonstrate that there exists frequency-domain Berry curvature in the wave function of photons in dispersive optical systems. This property arises from the frequency dispersion of its dielectric function, which makes Maxwell equations a nonstandard eigenvalue equation, with the eigenvalue (freque…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066301] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Frequency-Domain Berry Curvature Effect on Time Refraction
item.dc:creator: Shiyue Deng, Yang Gao, and Qian Niu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Intervalley-Coupled Twisted Bilayer Graphene from Substrate Commensuration
item.description:
Author(s): Bo-Ting Chen, Michael G. Scheer, and Biao Lian

We show that intervalley coupling can be induced in twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) by aligning the bottom graphene layer with either of two types of commensurate insulating triangular Bravais lattice substrate. The intervalley coupling folds the $±K$ valleys of TBG to the $\mathrm{Γ}$-point and hybr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066401] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bo-Ting Chen, Michael G. Scheer, and Biao Lian

We show that intervalley coupling can be induced in twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) by aligning the bottom graphene layer with either of two types of commensurate insulating triangular Bravais lattice substrate. The intervalley coupling folds the ±K valleys of TBG to the Γ-point and hybridizes the or…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066401] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Intervalley-Coupled Twisted Bilayer Graphene from Substrate Commensuration
item.dc:creator: Bo-Ting Chen, Michael G. Scheer, and Biao Lian
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Topology of Ultralocalized Insulators and Superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Bastien Lapierre, Luka Trifunovic, Titus Neupert, and Piet W. Brouwer

The topology of an insulator can be defined even when all eigenstates of the system are localized—an extreme case of Anderson insulators that we call ultralocalized. We derive the classification of such ultralocalized insulators in all symmetry classes and dimensions. We clarify their bulk-boundary …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066601] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bastien Lapierre, Luka Trifunovic, Titus Neupert, and Piet W. Brouwer

The topology of an insulator can be defined even when all eigenstates of the system are localized—an extreme case of Anderson insulators that we call ultralocalized. We derive the classification of such ultralocalized insulators in all symmetry classes and dimensions. We clarify their bulk-boundary …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066601] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Topology of Ultralocalized Insulators and Superconductors
item.dc:creator: Bastien Lapierre, Luka Trifunovic, Titus Neupert, and Piet W. Brouwer
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Realization of a Bulk-Insulating Ferromagnetic Topological Crystalline Insulator and Its Multicarrier Surface Dirac-Cone Transport
item.description:
Author(s): Yoshihiro Fukushima, Satoru Ichinkura, Taisuke Sasaki, and Toru Hirahara

We succeeded in fabricating an ideal, bulk-insulating ferromagnetic topological crystalline insulator by doping Mn to SnTe films grown on ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{3}$. The cancellation of the polarity of SnTe surface and electron doping due to Bi and Mn leads to an ideal case where only the …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066602] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yoshihiro Fukushima, Satoru Ichinkura, Taisuke Sasaki, and Toru Hirahara

We succeeded in fabricating an ideal, bulk-insulating ferromagnetic topological crystalline insulator by doping Mn to SnTe films grown on Bi2Te3. The cancellation of the polarity of SnTe surface and electron doping due to Bi and Mn leads to an ideal case where only the electron and holes of topologi…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066602] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Realization of a Bulk-Insulating Ferromagnetic Topological Crystalline Insulator and Its Multicarrier Surface Dirac-Cone Transport
item.dc:creator: Yoshihiro Fukushima, Satoru Ichinkura, Taisuke Sasaki, and Toru Hirahara
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Experimental Measurement of Negative Grain-Boundary Triple Line Tension
item.description:
Author(s): Xiuming Xiao and Ziren Wang

We measured negative grain-boundary triple line tension within bulk hard-sphere colloidal polycrystals. Compared to the grain boundaries, the tension is predominantly distributed in the grain lattices. The negative tension drives finite premelting at triple junctions and leads to a marginally denser…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 068201] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiuming Xiao and Ziren Wang

We measured negative grain-boundary triple line tension within bulk hard-sphere colloidal polycrystals. Compared to the grain boundaries, the tension is predominantly distributed in the grain lattices. The negative tension drives finite premelting at triple junctions and leads to a marginally denser…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 068201] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Experimental Measurement of Negative Grain-Boundary Triple Line Tension
item.dc:creator: Xiuming Xiao and Ziren Wang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Polymers, Chemical Physics, Soft Matter, and Biological Physics
item.prism:section: Polymers, Chemical Physics, Soft Matter, and Biological Physics

item.title: Space Charge Drives Electromechanical Conversion in Ion-Implanted Polymers via an Apparent Piezoelectric Effect
item.description:
Author(s): Andris Šutka, Holger Fiedler, Artis Linarts, Kaspars Mālnieks, Kaspars Pudzs, Joseph D. Berry, and Peter C. Sherrell

Ion implantation is a powerful tool to modify material chemistry and structure. The implantation process was considered to result in a net-neutral material, due to implanted ionic charge being compensated by the host materials lattice. Here, we show ion implantation into polytetrafluoroethylene (PTF…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056203] Published Fri Feb 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Andris Šutka, Holger Fiedler, Artis Linarts, Kaspars Mālnieks, Kaspars Pudzs, Joseph D. Berry, and Peter C. Sherrell

Ion implantation is a powerful tool to modify material chemistry and structure. The implantation process was considered to result in a net-neutral material, due to implanted ionic charge being compensated by the host materials lattice. Here, we show ion implantation into polytetrafluoroethylene (PTF…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056203] Published Fri Feb 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Space Charge Drives Electromechanical Conversion in Ion-Implanted Polymers via an Apparent Piezoelectric Effect
item.dc:creator: Andris Šutka, Holger Fiedler, Artis Linarts, Kaspars Mālnieks, Kaspars Pudzs, Joseph D. Berry, and Peter C. Sherrell
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Conformal Scalar Field Theory from Ising Tricriticality on the Fuzzy Sphere
item.description:
Author(s): Joseph Taylor, Cristian Voinea, Zlatko Papić, and Ruihua Fan

Free theories are landmarks in the landscape of quantum field theories: their exact solvability serves as a pillar for perturbative constructions of interacting theories. Fuzzy sphere regularization, which combines quantum Hall physics with state-operator correspondence, has recently been proposed a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056503] Published Fri Feb 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Joseph Taylor, Cristian Voinea, Zlatko Papić, and Ruihua Fan

Free theories are landmarks in the landscape of quantum field theories: their exact solvability serves as a pillar for perturbative constructions of interacting theories. Fuzzy sphere regularization, which combines quantum Hall physics with state-operator correspondence, has recently been proposed a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056503] Published Fri Feb 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Conformal Scalar Field Theory from Ising Tricriticality on the Fuzzy Sphere
item.dc:creator: Joseph Taylor, Cristian Voinea, Zlatko Papić, and Ruihua Fan
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Nonlinear Faraday Rotation of Light Polarization in Time-Reversal-Invariant Materials
item.description:
Author(s): Falko Pientka and Inti Sodemann Villadiego

We investigate the propagation of electromagnetic waves through materials displaying a nonlinear Hall effect. The coupled Maxwell-Boltzmann equations for traveling waves can be mapped onto ordinary differential equations that resemble those for the motion of a pendulum. In the weakly nonlinear regim…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056901] Published Fri Feb 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Falko Pientka and Inti Sodemann Villadiego

We investigate the propagation of electromagnetic waves through materials displaying a nonlinear Hall effect. The coupled Maxwell-Boltzmann equations for traveling waves can be mapped onto ordinary differential equations that resemble those for the motion of a pendulum. In the weakly nonlinear regim…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056901] Published Fri Feb 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Nonlinear Faraday Rotation of Light Polarization in Time-Reversal-Invariant Materials
item.dc:creator: Falko Pientka and Inti Sodemann Villadiego
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Nonreciprocal Wave-Mediated Interactions Power a Classical Time Crystal
item.description:
Author(s): Mia C. Morrell, Leela Elliott, and David G. Grier

A pair of acoustically levitated beads powered by nonreciprocal interactions can spontaneously organize into a continuous time crystal, a state of matter that sustains steady-state oscillations without periodic driving.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 057201] Published Fri Feb 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mia C. Morrell, Leela Elliott, and David G. Grier

A pair of acoustically levitated beads powered by nonreciprocal interactions can spontaneously organize into a continuous time crystal, a state of matter that sustains steady-state oscillations without periodic driving.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 057201] Published Fri Feb 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Nonreciprocal Wave-Mediated Interactions Power a Classical Time Crystal
item.dc:creator: Mia C. Morrell, Leela Elliott, and David G. Grier
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Statistical Physics; Classical, Nonlinear, and Complex Systems
item.prism:section: Statistical Physics; Classical, Nonlinear, and Complex Systems
